State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
File 
Dote 
No. : 
June 16, 1983 
T-83-150 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Subject: 
Bob Leidigh 
Telephone Advice to Brenton Bleier, Attorney for 
Sacramento Cable TV Commission and to Jim Jackson, 
City Attorney, Sacramento, re: 
Gov. Code Section 84308 
On June 14 and 15, 1983, I responded to telephone requests 
for advice by Mr. Bleier (pronounced "Blair") who is attorney for 
the Sacramento Cable TV Commission. 
I also subsequently 
responded to a telephone request for advice by Jim Jackson, City 
Attorney of Sacramento, relative to the same subject, also on 
June 15, 1983. 
Initially (6/14), Mr. Bleier wanted to know what, if any, 
interpretations we had maae of Section 84308. 
I advised him that 
we had issued a set of regulations and some advice letters and 
that we t1ave an opinion pending before the Commission on LAFCOs 
as to whether they are covered. 
Mr. Bleier stated tt1at the CTVC 
was made up of elected officials serving on a joint powers agency 
to award the cable TV franchise for Sacramento. 
Members include 
county supervisors, and city councilmembers (Sacramento and 
Galt). 
We discussed the type of decision whict1 tt1e CTVC makes 
and concluded that it was quite probably a license or an 
entitlement for use. 
I suggested that he obtain a copy of our 
regulations and review them so that he could ask more specific 
questions and we arranged his pick up of a copy. 
He also asked about the penalties and remedies for 
violations. 
I explained that generally tt1ey are the same as for 
other violations of tt1e Act; however, Section 9l003(b) does not 
apply as it is limited to Chapter 7 violations. 
In our second conversation ( 
5), Mr. Bleier asked whether 
it would be cons 
red a contribut 
if it were on 
a 
to 
n. 
We looked at tne r 
s and at 18215, tnen at 82015 
at 84 
6, wh 
makes it c 
r that a loan (unless 
from a commercial lenaing institution in the ordinary course of 
is 
contribut 
unless not 
it 


Ie 
June 16, 1983 
Page 2 
He then wanted to know when the 30-day period for returning 
contributions began to run. 
Is it tne latter of the two 
discoverles, i.e., when you learn of the application and when you 
learn of the contribution. 
After consultation with Barbara and 
Janis, I advised him that it runs from the latter of the two and 
that knowledge of the contribution is as aefined in 18438.7(c), 
i.e., disclosure by the contributor or actual knowledge by the 
member. 
Mr. Jackson joined Mr. Cardoza of his staff in their call 
aSking WhY we thought that the CTVC was covered at all. 
They 
contend that the statute (84308) limits its application to quasi-
judicial boards or commissions and that the awarding of a 
franchise by CTVC is a legislative act and the only thing it 
does; therefore, since it does nothing which is quasi-jud lal, 
it can't be considered a quasi-judicial board or commission and 
can't be covered. 
I advised him that our pOSition has been that 
bodies which are not exempted under Section 84308(c) as 
legislative are covered whenever they aeal with applications for 
permits, licenses or other entitlements for use. 
The regs 
(18438.2) make it clear that the cable TV franchise award is such 
a decis 
and therefore it is covered. 
He sa 
that his quarrel 
was not witn the statute but with our regulatlons. 
He feels that 
only 1094.S-reviewable actions should be covered and that the 
CTVC actlons are not. 
I advised him to put his request in 
writing and told him of our pending opinioni regarding LAFCOs and 
whether tney are covered. 
I advised him as to when tne next 
hearing on the opinion will be (7/12/83). 
BL:pln

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Sacramento Cable TV Commission (CTVC), a joint powers agency of elected officials awarding cable TV franchises, is covered under Government Code Section 84308, and what contribution-related obligations and penalties apply to its members.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the CTVC is covered under Section 84308 because it deals with applications for licenses and entitlements for use, even though it performs legislative functions. Members must return contributions within 30 days of the later of learning of the application or the contribution, with knowledge defined by Section 18438.7(c).
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document is internal FPPC memorandum of telephone advice rather than formal response letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; text is narrative summary of telephone conversations. OCR quality issues in portions of text (incomplete words, unclear passages). Multiple parties advised on same subject matter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC concluded that the CTVC is covered under Section 84308 because it deals with applications for licenses and entitlements for use, even though it performs legislative functions. Members must return contributions within 30 days of the later of learning of the application or the contribution, with knowledge defined by Section 18438.7(c).

embedding.summary: This memorandum documents telephone advice given by the FPPC to counsel for the Sacramento Cable TV Commission and the City Attorney of Sacramento regarding Section 84308 coverage, contribution obligations, and the scope of quasi-judicial authority.
